story: Autonomous firefighting drones (Anduril, AURA Foresight, Dryad Networks) reached the finals of the $11M XPRIZE Wildfire competition, live-tested June 2026 near Nenana, Alaska in partnership with UAF's ACUASI. Winner to be announced September 2026.
dedupe_check: FRESH (re-tested with distinctive entity set; generic-token overlap with 07-11 wildfire-smoke-forecast story on 'uaf'/'wildfire' is not a real duplicate)

numbers.text: $11 million total XPRIZE Wildfire prize purse
numbers.label: primary

numbers.text: $1 million Lockheed Martin "Accurate Detection Intelligence" Bonus Prize
numbers.label: primary

numbers.text: Autonomous Wildfire Response track: detect and suppress across a simulated area of about 1,000 square kilometers within 10 minutes
numbers.label: primary, competition spec not literal test-site footprint

numbers.text: Finals live-tested over two weeks in June 2026 near Nenana, Alaska, in partnership with UAF's ACUASI, which managed the airspace at Nenana Municipal Airport
numbers.source: XPRIZE finals-testing page + Alaska Legislature doc 10959
numbers.label: primary + government

numbers.text: Three teams in the autonomous-response final test: Anduril, AURA Foresight, Dryad Networks
numbers.source: XPRIZE (Anduril confirmed only via this primary source, no independent press names Anduril)
numbers.label: primary-only for Anduril

numbers.text: Winner to be announced September 2026, undecided as of publication
numbers.label: corroborated

numbers.text: Silvanet sensors detect VOCs, carbon monoxide, and PM2.5; Silvaguard drone carries up to 100 liters of suppressant
numbers.label: company estimate

numbers.text: Up to 3 controlled test fires per session, no human intervention
numbers.label: primary

numbers.text: More than 1 million acres burned in Alaska in 2025
numbers.source: Alaska Interagency Coordination Center / Alaska Beacon
numbers.label: primary/agency

banned_numbers: 1.68 million acres (XPRIZE-only estimate that doesn't survive primary check; use 'more than 1 million')
8-hour drone flight endurance (no source; only the competition's 8-hour monitoring window is sourced)
'zero humans on scene' as a verbatim quote (use 'no human intervention')

quotes.text: Detecting fires within minutes and extinguishing them before they become catastrophic is no longer a vision for the future. It is a reality.
quotes.speaker: Carsten Brinkschulte, Dryad Networks CEO

quotes.text: To stand among the final three teams in the world and demonstrate a fully autonomous system that can detect and respond to wildfire ignitions within minutes validates the technology, vision, and dedication of our entire team.
quotes.speaker: Carsten Brinkschulte, Dryad Networks CEO

fact_check_safe_set.honest_caveat: This was a controlled competition demo on a managed FAA test range with small, deliberately-set test fires, not a real fast-moving uncontrolled wildfire in wind and smoke. No team has won yet; the winner is announced in September 2026. None of these systems has been proven suppressing a real Alaska wildfire at landscape scale.
